title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Advantages and Limitations of Term Insurance
Planning for the future is a key to ensure  financial security for your family. When you look for life insurance plans, it is important to be educated about them. Term insurance is a type of life insurance that is used for financial protection.  It provides sum assured to the nominee, which can be used for living expenses or other needs.  if you die within the duration of the policy. However, like any other financial product, it has some advantages and limitations.
In this blog, we will explore both sides thoroughly and what you need to consider prior to making a decision.
What is Term Insurance?
T Term insurance is a form of life insurance plan that provides coverage to the life assured for a certain number of years. If the life assured dies while the term insurance policy is active, the nominee receives a payout called the "sum assured".
Term insurance has no savings or maturity value. If you survive the term, you get nothing at the end of the term, unless you purchase a term plan with return of premium feature.
Let us now look at the advantages and limitations of these policies.
Advantages of Term Insurance
Term insurance plans offer many benefits. Here are the main advantages of term insurance:
Affordable premiums: The premiums are usually low compared to other life insurance products.
High coverage amount: You can get a cover at a reasonable cost.
Flexible terms: You can choose suitable coverage.
Tax benefits: Premiums are eligible for tax benefits under Section 80C, if opted for old tax regime, and the payout is tax-free under Section 10(10D), subject to certain terms and conditions.
Easy to buy: Most term insurance plans can also be purchased online.
Additional protection: You can add riders like critical illness, accidental death, waiver of premium, etc.
Peace of mind: It ensures your family’s financial future is secure.
Limitations of Term Insurance
Even though it has many pros, there are some limitations of term insurance too:
No maturity benefit: If you survive the term, you do not get any amount unless you choose a return of premium option.
Pure protection: There is no savings or return component.
Fixed term:  Once the term expires, the policy terminates without any payout, except for TROP plans
These are the common advantages and limitations of term insurance plans and things to consider while buying.
Who Should Buy Term Insurance?
A term insurance plan is a good option for:
People who have dependents (spouse, children, or parents)
People with loans or liabilities
It is especially useful for those who prefer protection over savings, at an affordable premium. The plan can also be a part of your insurance and investment planning.
Why People Consider Term Insurance?
Some people buy term insurance for these reasons:
To protect their family’s daily needs
To cover ongoing loans like home loans
To help children continue their education
To provide peace of mind
Any other life goal
It is an affordable way to plan your future. It gives you the comfort of knowing your family’s finances will be taken care of after your death.
Tips for Choosing the Right Term Plan
Here are some helpful tips:
Start early: If you buy a plan early, the premiums might be lower.
Check the claim settlement ratio: Check how many claims the insurer has settled in a given year out of claims received in that year.
Compare features: Not all plans are the same. Check options before finalizing.
Choose the right sum assured: It should cover your family’s needs in the future.
Add riders if needed: These give extra benefits on the term plan.
These tips can help you find the best term insurance plan for your loved ones.
How to Get the Right Term Insurance Coverage for Your Needs?
Getting the right term insurance coverage means choosing a plan that fits your family’s future needs. Everyone has different goals, expenses, and responsibilities. The right coverage should be enough to take care of your family’s daily expenses, loans, and future plans in your absence. Here’s how you can do it easily.
Assess Your Financial Needs
Before choosing a plan, think about how much your family would need every month if you're not there.
Daily expenses: Food, rent, transport, electricity, school fees, etc.
Loans: Home loan, personal loan, car loan, or credit card bills.
Future goals: Children’s college fees, marriage, and the health needs of your parents.
Emergency fund: For sudden medical or home expenses.
Add all these to get an idea of how much money your family may need over the next 10–30 years.
Calculate the Sum Assured
Determining the right sum assured takes into account various factors including, the value of your income, the number of dependents on it, any outstanding liabilities you may have incurred, your future financial goals.
Choose Policy Term
Select a term that suits your needs
If you plan to retire at 60, and you are 30 now, you can choose a 30-year term.
Also, match it to your biggest responsibilities (like children’s education or loan repayment period).
Compare Premiums and Features
Use online tools to compare the term insurance cost, riders (like critical illness, waiver of premium, etc), claim options, and premium payment modes. Go for a plan that fits your budget and aligns with your life goals.
Check Claim Settlement Ratio
Always check the insurer’s claim settlement ratio.
A good claim settlement ratio shows how many claims are paid by the insurance company.
This will allow your family to enjoy the complete benefit promised to them by the insurer.
Conclusion
A term insurance plan is an effective way to provide your loved ones with financial backup for difficult times.  It provides affordable financial protection for your family in case of your demise within the policy tenure. The claim amount can help your family cover expenses like daily needs, loans, and future goals. While it does not offer savings or maturity benefits, its usually low premiums make it a practical choice for many. By carefully assessing your needs, comparing plans, and considering factors like claim settlement ratios and riders, you can select a term insurance plan that fits your budget and offers peace of mind. Alongside this, a term insurance plan can also avail you tax benefits as per Section 80C (under the old tax regime). Integrating term insurance into your overall financial plan helps secure your family’s future effectively.
FAQs
What is the scope of term insurance?
Term insurance provides financial support to your family if you pass away during the policy period. It helps cover expenses like daily needs, loans, school fees, and emergencies to keep your family financially secure.
Is term insurance permanent?
No, term insurance covers you for a fixed number of years, such as 20, 30, or 40 years. If you outlive the term, the coverage ends with no payout. For lifelong coverage, consider a whole life policy.
Does term insurance cover death?
Yes, term insurance pays a lump sum or regular payout to your nominee if you pass away during the policy term, helping them manage expenses and stay financially stable.
Which term insurance is best?
The best term insurance plan fits your coverage needs, budget, and offers good claim settlement. Look for affordable premiums, useful riders, and a reliable insurer with a strong claim record.
Can a person take 2 term insurance policies?
Yes, you can have multiple term insurance policies for extra coverage. Just inform each insurer about your other policies to avoid claim issues later.
What is the right age to buy term insurance?
The best age to buy term insurance is in your 20s or early 30s when premiums are low and you’re usually healthy. You can still buy it later, but the costs may be higher. Buying term insurance as soon as you have dependents to protect might be a wise decision.
Disclaimer
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. full_pdf_text:
Advantages and Limitations of Term Insurance
Planning for the future is a key to ensure financial security for your family. When you look for life insurance plans, it is important to be educated about them. Term insurance is a type of life insurance that is used for financial protection. It provides sum assured to the nominee, which can be used for living expenses or other needs. if you die within the duration of the policy. However, like any other financial product, it has some advantages and limitations.
In this blog, we will explore both sides thoroughly and what you need to consider prior to making a decision.
What is Term Insurance?
Term insurance is a life insurance plan that provides life coverage to the life assured for the policy tenure . If the life assured dies while the term insurance policy is active, the nominee receives a payout called the "sum assured".
Term insurance has no savings or maturity value. If you survive the term, you dont get death benefit, unless you purchase a term plan with return of premium feature.
Let us now look at the advantages and limitations of these policies.
Advantages of Term Insurance
Term insurance plans offer many benefits. Here are the main advantages of term insurance:
● Affordable premiums: The premiums are usually low compared to other life insurance products. ● High coverage amount: You can get a cover at a reasonable cost. ● Flexible terms: You can choose suitable coverage. ● Tax benefits: Premiums are eligible for tax benefits under Section 80C, if opted for old tax regime, and the payout is tax-free under Section 10(10D), subject to certain terms and conditions. ● Additional protection: You can add riders like critical illness benefit rider, accidental death benefit rider, waiver of premium rider, etc at a nominal additional premium. ● Peace of mind: It ensures your family’s financial future is secure.
Limitations of Term Insurance
Even though it has many pros, there are some limitations of term insurance too:
● No maturity benefit: If you survive the term, you do not get any amount unless you choose a return of premium option. ● Pure protection: There is no savings or return component.
● Fixed term: Once the term expires, the policy terminates without any payout, except for TROP plans
These are the common advantages and limitations of term insurance plans and things to consider while buying.
Who Should Buy Term Insurance?
A term insurance plan can be considered a suitable option for:
● People who have dependents (spouse, children, or parents)
● People with loans or liabilities
It is especially useful for those who prefer protection, at an affordable premium. The plan can also be a part of your insurance and investment planning.
Why People Consider Term Insurance?
Some people buy term insurance for these reasons:
● To protect their family’s daily financial needs ● To cover ongoing loans like home loans by ensuring that in case of the life assured's death, the nominee(s) can pay off the outstanding home loan with the help of the death benefit, relieving the family of financial burden ● To help children continue their education ● To provide peace of mind ● Any other life goal
It is an affordable way to plan your future. It gives you the comfort of knowing your family’s finances will be taken care of after your death.
Tips for Choosing the Right Term Plan
Here are some helpful tips:
● Start early: If you buy a plan early, the premiums might be lower. ● Check the claim settlement ratio: The claim settlement ratio is the percentage which indicates the number of claims settled by an Insurer in a financial year in comparison to the claim received. A high claim settlement ratio is usually an indication that the insurer settles claims efficiently..
● Compare features: Not all plans are the same. Check options before finalizing. ● Choose the right sum assured: It should cover your family’s needs in the future. ● Add riders if needed: These give extra benefits on the term plan, at an additional nominal premium.
These tips can help you find the best term insurance plan for your loved ones.
How to Get the Right Term Insurance Coverage for Your Needs?
Getting the right term insurance coverage means choosing a plan that fits your family’s future needs. Everyone has different goals, expenses, and responsibilities. The right coverage should be enough to take care of your family’s daily expenses, loans, and future plans in your absence. Here’s how you can do it easily.
Assess Your Financial Needs
Before choosing a plan, think about how much your family would need every month if you're not there.
● Daily expenses: Food, rent, transport, electricity, school fees, etc. ● Loans: Home loan, personal loan, car loan, or credit card bills. ● Future goals: Children’s college fees, marriage, and the health needs of your parents. ● Emergency fund: For sudden medical or home expenses.
Add all these to get an idea of how much money your family may need over the next 10–30 years.
Calculate the Sum Assured
Determining the right sum assured takes into account various factors including, the value of your income, the number of dependents on it, any outstanding liabilities you may have incurred, your future financial goals.
Choose the Policy Term
Select a term that suits your financial needs
● If you plan to retire at 60, and you are 30 now, you can choose a 30-year term.
● Also, match it to your responsibilities (like children’s education or loan repayment period).
Compare Premiums and Features
Use online tools to compare the term insurance cost, riders (like critical illness, waiver of premium, etc), claim options, and premium payment modes. Go for a plan that fits your budget and aligns with your life goals.
Check Claim Settlement Ratio
Always check the insurer’s claim settlement ratio.
● Claim settlement ratio indicates the number of claims settles by the Insurer in a financial year in comparison to the claims received. A high claim settlement ratio typically indicates efficient settlement of claims .
● This will allow your family to enjoy the complete benefit promised to them by the insurer.
Conclusion
A term insurance plan is an effective way to provide your loved ones with financial backup for difficult times. It provides affordable financial protection for your family in case of your demise within the policy tenure. The sum assured can help your family cover expenses like daily needs, other financial obligations , future goals etc. While it does not offer savings or maturity benefits, its usually low premiums make it a practical choice for many. By carefully assessing your financial needs, comparing plans, and considering factors like claim settlement ratios and riders, you can select a term insurance plan that fits your budget and offers peace of mind. Alongside this, a term insurance plan can also avail you tax benefits as per Section 80C (under the old tax regime). Integrating term insurance into your overall financial plan helps secure your family’s future effectively.
FAQs
What is the scope of term insurance?
Term insurance provides financial support to your family if you pass away during the policy period. It helps cover expenses like daily needs, loans, school fees, and emergencies to keep your family financially secure.
Is term insurance permanent?
No, term insurance covers you for a fixed number of years, such as 20, 30, or 40 years. If you outlive the term, the coverage ends with no payout. For lifelong coverage, consider a whole life policy.
Does term insurance cover death?
Yes, term insurance pays a lump sum or regular payout to your nominee if you pass away during the policy term, helping them manage expenses and stay financially stable.
Which term insurance is best?
The best term insurance plan fits your coverage needs, budget, and offers smooth claim settlement. Look for affordable premiums, useful riders, and a reliable insurer with a high claim settlement ratio.
Can a person take 2 term insurance policies?
Yes, you can have multiple term insurance policies for extra coverage. Just inform each insurer about your other policies to avoid potential claim issues later.
What is the right age to buy term insurance?
The best age to buy term insurance is in your 20s or early 30s when premiums are typically low and you’re healthy. You can still buy it later, but the premiums may be higher.
Disclaimer The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
